settings.name: blend
settings.description: Blends an image onto another using a blend mode.
settings.help:
Walks every background pixel, samples the foreground at the same coordinate minus the position offset, and composites it using the selected BlendMode (Over, Lerp, Multiply, Overlay, SoftLight, and 12 more). Math is performed in the chosen ColorSpace; all 14 spaces (sRGB, Linear RGB, HSL, HSV, HWB, Lab, LCH, Oklab, Oklch, CMYK, XYZ, xyY, YCbCr, YUV) are supported and results are converted back to sRGBA for storage.

The amount input provides a global opacity; the alpha image (per-pixel, RGB channels averaged) multiplies that opacity so you can restrict the blend with a mask. Source alpha channels feed into the blend math. Foreground pixels outside the background's bounds leave the background untouched. Output size is taken from the background; positions may be negative to shift the foreground past the top-left edge.

inputs.name: background
inputs.description: Base image the foreground is composited onto; sets the output size.

inputs.name: foreground
inputs.description: Image composited on top of the background using the chosen blend mode.

inputs.name: amount
inputs.description: Global opacity applied to the blended foreground; 0 shows only the background.

inputs.name: alpha
inputs.description: Optional mask image; its per-pixel RGB average multiplies the blend amount.

inputs.name: blend mode
inputs.description: Compositing formula used to combine foreground and background.

inputs.name: color space
inputs.description: Colour space the blend math runs in (sRGB, Linear, HSL, Lab, etc.).

inputs.name: position x
inputs.description: Horizontal offset in pixels from the background's origin to place the foreground.

inputs.name: position y
inputs.description: Vertical offset in pixels from the background's origin to place the foreground.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Composited RGBA image sized to match the background.

settings.name: transform
settings.description: Translate, rotate, and scale an image, choosing how to fill the exposed space.
settings.help:
One affine transform applied in a single resample: the image is scaled and rotated about its centre, then translated by `offset x` / `offset y`. Offsets are fractions of the image size (0.25 = a quarter of the way across, positive x right, positive y down), so the same value gives the same shift at any resolution — change the image from 512px to 4096px and you don't touch the offsets. `rotation` is in degrees, positive turns clockwise. `scale x` / `scale y` scale about the centre (1 = no change); the canvas size stays fixed. Doing all three in one node keeps the result sharp — chaining separate translate/rotate/scale nodes resamples the image each time and softens it.

`edge` chooses what appears in the space the transform uncovers:
• fill — a solid `fill color` (default fully transparent)
• wrap — the image tiles, so anything that moves off one side reappears on the other (keeps a tileable input tileable)
• extend — the border pixels are stretched out to the edge
• mirror — the image is reflected back across each edge

Sampling is bilinear, so whole-pixel/whole-degree transforms stay crisp and fractional ones interpolate. Output dimensions and channel count match the input; for images with fewer than 4 channels the fill colour is reduced to match (luminance for 1 channel, RGB for 3).

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Source image to transform.

inputs.name: offset x
inputs.description: Horizontal shift as a fraction of image width (0.25 = a quarter across); positive moves content right. Resolution-independent.

inputs.name: offset y
inputs.description: Vertical shift as a fraction of image height; positive moves content down. Resolution-independent.

inputs.name: rotation
inputs.description: Rotation in degrees about the image centre; positive is clockwise.

inputs.name: scale x
inputs.description: Horizontal scale about the centre; 1 = unchanged.

inputs.name: scale y
inputs.description: Vertical scale about the centre; 1 = unchanged.

inputs.name: edge
inputs.description: What fills the exposed space: fill colour, wrap, extend, or mirror.

inputs.name: fill color
inputs.description: Colour used for exposed space when edge = fill (default transparent).

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: The transformed image, same size and channel count as the input.

settings.name: outline
settings.description: Stroke a mask edge with a configurable colour and thickness.
settings.help:
Threshold the input to a binary mask (>= 0.5 on luminance), then run morphological dilation and erosion to grow a thickened and shrunken copy; the difference between them defines the ring. The position input chooses which difference: `0` = outer stroke (dilate − mask), `1` = inner stroke (mask − erode), `2` = centred stroke (dilate − erode) straddling the original edge.

Output is always 4-channel RGBA with alpha matching the ring strength. The ring is fully opaque where the dilate/erode difference is saturated; antialiased samples that sit between 0 and 1 pass through to the alpha channel so the stroke has soft edges by default. Use `blit` or `blend` to composite the outline on top of the source image.

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Source image or mask whose edges to stroke.

inputs.name: thickness
inputs.description: Stroke width in pixels (radius of dilate / erode).

inputs.name: position
inputs.description: 0 = outer, 1 = inner, 2 = centred around the edge.

inputs.name: color
inputs.description: Color used for the outline strokes.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: RGBA image containing only the outline; alpha is zero where the source mask is unchanged.

settings.name: transform

settings.help:

Offsets are fractions of the image size (0.25 = a quarter of the way across, positive x right, positive y down), so the same value gives the same shift at any resolution — change the image from 512px to 4096px and you don't touch the offsets. `rotation` is in degrees, positive turns clockwise. `scale x` / `scale y` scale about the centre (1 = no change); the canvas size stays fixed. `edge` chooses what appears in the space the transform uncovers:
• fill — a solid `fill color` (default fully transparent)
• wrap — the image tiles, so anything that moves off one side reappears on the other (keeps a tileable input tileable)

• mirror — the image is reflected back across each edge

inputs.description: Source image to transform.

inputs.name: offset x
inputs.description: Horizontal shift as a fraction of image width (0.25 = a quarter across); positive moves content right. Resolution-independent.

inputs.name: offset y
inputs.description: Vertical shift as a fraction of image height; positive moves content down. Resolution-independent.

inputs.name: rotation
inputs.description: Rotation in degrees about the image centre; positive is clockwise.

inputs.name: scale x
inputs.description: Horizontal scale about the centre; 1 = unchanged.

inputs.name: scale y
inputs.description: Vertical scale about the centre; 1 = unchanged.

inputs.name: edge
inputs.description: What fills the exposed space: fill colour, wrap, extend, or mirror.

inputs.name: fill color
inputs.description: Colour used for exposed space when edge = fill (default transparent).

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: The transformed image, same size and channel count as the input.

• mirror — the image is reflected back across each edge

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Source image to transform.

inputs.name: offset x
inputs.description: Horizontal shift as a fraction of image width (0.25 = a quarter across); positive moves content right. Resolution-independent.

inputs.name: offset y
inputs.description: Vertical shift as a fraction of image height; positive moves content down. Resolution-independent.

inputs.name: rotation
inputs.description: Rotation in degrees about the image centre; positive is clockwise.

inputs.name: scale x
inputs.description: Horizontal scale about the centre; 1 = unchanged.

inputs.name: scale y
inputs.description: Vertical scale about the centre; 1 = unchanged.

inputs.name: edge
inputs.description: What fills the exposed space: fill colour, wrap, extend, or mirror.

inputs.name: fill color
inputs.description: Colour used for exposed space when edge = fill (default transparent).

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: The transformed image, same size and channel count as the input.

settings.name: integer
settings.description: An integer number input.
settings.help:
Emits a single signed 32-bit integer onto the graph. Use this node to supply counts, indices, seeds, or other whole-number parameters.

Connections from decimal inputs are truncated toward zero (not rounded); booleans become 0 or 1. Values outside the i32 range cannot be represented and will be clamped or error during conversion.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Integer value to emit; decimals are truncated to integers.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: The integer value from the input, passed through.

settings.name: circle
settings.description: Generates a filled circle as a grayscale SDF.
settings.help:
Rasterises a filled circle into a 1-channel FloatImage by evaluating a signed distance function in pixel space and applying smoothstep anti-aliasing at a one-and-a-half-pixel edge width. Output is 1.0 inside the disc and 0.0 outside.

radius is normalised so that 1.0 spans half of the shorter image dimension, which keeps the shape perfectly round even on non-square canvases (the ellipse node, by contrast, normalises each axis independently). center_x and center_y offset the disc from the middle in units of half the canvas (0 = centred, 1 = the corresponding edge). Handy as an alpha matte or mask for blend nodes.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description: Width of the generated image in pixels.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description: Height of the generated image in pixels.

inputs.name: radius
inputs.description: Circle radius; 1.0 spans half the shorter image dimension.

inputs.name: center_x
inputs.description: Horizontal center offset from the middle, in units of half the canvas width.

inputs.name: center_y
inputs.description: Vertical center offset from the middle, in units of half the canvas height.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Grayscale mask with the circle filled white on a black background.

settings.name: divide
settings.description: Divides two numbers.
settings.help:
Computes a / b for integer or decimal inputs. Mixed integer/decimal types promote to decimal, while two integers stay in integer arithmetic with truncation toward zero.

Setting b to zero raises a division-by-zero error rather than producing infinity or NaN, so the node surfaces the problem clearly instead of silently propagating a bad value downstream.

inputs.name: a
inputs.description: Dividend (numerator) of the division.

inputs.name: b
inputs.description: Divisor (denominator); produces an error when set to zero.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Quotient a / b; integer inputs truncate toward zero.

settings.name: resize exact
settings.description: Resizes an image to the exact width and height.
settings.help:
Stretches or squashes the image so it matches the requested width and height exactly, without regard to aspect ratio. If the target aspect differs from the source, the image will appear distorted; prefer `resize` (aspect-preserving fit) or `resize fill` (aspect-preserving cover + crop) when that matters.

The filter type selects the resampling kernel (Nearest for blocky up-scaling, Lanczos3 for sharp photographic results, etc.). Internally the image is round-tripped through DynamicImage, so the output follows the image crate's channel conventions.

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Source image to resize.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description: Exact output width in pixels; may distort aspect ratio.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description: Exact output height in pixels; may distort aspect ratio.

inputs.name: filter type
inputs.description: Resampling filter used for the scale.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Image resized to exactly the requested dimensions.

outputs.name: width
outputs.description: Output width in pixels.

outputs.name: height
outputs.description: Output height in pixels.

settings.name: integer
settings.description: An integer number input.
settings.help:
Emits a single signed 32-bit integer onto the graph. Use this node to supply counts, indices, seeds, or other whole-number parameters.

Connections from decimal inputs are truncated toward zero (not rounded); booleans become 0 or 1. Values outside the i32 range cannot be represented and will be clamped or error during conversion.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Integer value to emit; decimals are truncated to integers.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: The integer value from the input, passed through.

settings.name: line
settings.description: Generates a line shape as a grayscale SDF.
settings.help:
Draws an anti-aliased line segment from (start_x, start_y) to (end_x, end_y) with rounded caps, by evaluating the signed distance to the segment and smoothstepping the edge. Coordinates are 0-1 fractions of the canvas; thickness is in normalised units.

Output is a 1-channel FloatImage mask with 1.0 inside the stroke and 0.0 outside. When start and end coincide the node degrades gracefully into a filled disc centred on that point rather than producing an empty image.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description: Width of the generated image in pixels.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description: Height of the generated image in pixels.

inputs.name: start_x
inputs.description: Start-point X as a 0-1 fraction of image width.

inputs.name: start_y
inputs.description: Start-point Y as a 0-1 fraction of image height.

inputs.name: end_x
inputs.description: End-point X as a 0-1 fraction of image width.

inputs.name: end_y
inputs.description: End-point Y as a 0-1 fraction of image height.

inputs.name: thickness
inputs.description: Thickness of the line in normalised units.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Grayscale mask with the line drawn white on a black background.

settings.name: to file
settings.description: Saves an image to a file.
settings.help:
Encodes the input image and writes it into the chosen folder under the given base filename; the extension is appended automatically based on the selected image format. The color format selector controls the output bit depth and channel layout (Gray8/16, GrayA8/16, Rgb8/16/32F, Rgba8/16/32F).

The quality slider applies to the lossy formats (JPEG and AVIF), and the png compression selector only to PNG (all PNG settings produce identical pixels — only file size and encode time differ). WebP is always encoded losslessly; Radiance HDR writes from the Rgb32F color format; the remaining formats have no encoder settings. Incompatible format/color-format combinations (for example an RGBA channel layout into a JPEG) are rejected before any file is written, and the full saved path is returned as an output for chaining.

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Image to encode and save to disk.

inputs.name: file name
inputs.description: Base filename for the saved file; extension is appended automatically.

inputs.name: folder
inputs.description: Destination folder where the image file will be written.

inputs.name: image format
inputs.description: Image container format (JPEG, PNG, etc.) that determines the extension.

inputs.name: quality
inputs.description: Lossy compression quality from 1 (smallest) to 100 (best); applies to JPEG and AVIF.

inputs.name: color format
inputs.description: Pixel encoding (bit depth and channel layout) used to write the file.

inputs.name: png compression
inputs.description: PNG compression effort (lossless; affects file size and encode time only). Ignored for other formats.

outputs.name: file path
outputs.description: Full path of the file that was written.

settings.name: blend
settings.description: Blends an image onto another using a blend mode.
settings.help:
Walks every background pixel, samples the foreground at the same coordinate minus the position offset, and composites it using the selected BlendMode (Over, Lerp, Multiply, Overlay, SoftLight, and 12 more). Math is performed in the chosen ColorSpace; all 14 spaces (sRGB, Linear RGB, HSL, HSV, HWB, Lab, LCH, Oklab, Oklch, CMYK, XYZ, xyY, YCbCr, YUV) are supported and results are converted back to sRGBA for storage.

The amount input provides a global opacity; the alpha image (per-pixel, RGB channels averaged) multiplies that opacity so you can restrict the blend with a mask. Source alpha channels feed into the blend math. Foreground pixels outside the background's bounds leave the background untouched. Output size is taken from the background; positions may be negative to shift the foreground past the top-left edge.

inputs.name: background
inputs.description: Base image the foreground is composited onto; sets the output size.

inputs.name: foreground
inputs.description: Image composited on top of the background using the chosen blend mode.

inputs.name: amount
inputs.description: Global opacity applied to the blended foreground; 0 shows only the background.

inputs.name: alpha
inputs.description: Optional mask image; its per-pixel RGB average multiplies the blend amount.

inputs.name: blend mode
inputs.description: Compositing formula used to combine foreground and background.

inputs.name: color space
inputs.description: Colour space the blend math runs in (sRGB, Linear, HSL, Lab, etc.).

inputs.name: position x
inputs.description: Horizontal offset in pixels from the background's origin to place the foreground.

inputs.name: position y
inputs.description: Vertical offset in pixels from the background's origin to place the foreground.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Composited RGBA image sized to match the background.

settings.name: from file
settings.description: Grabs an image from a file.
settings.help:
Decodes an image file from disk and converts it into a FloatImage, preserving the source channel count (grayscale stays 1ch, RGB 3ch, RGBA 4ch). The path input uses a picker filtered to the supported image extensions. JPEG XL files are decoded with jxl-oxide and PSD files with the psd crate (the flattened composite image; individual layers are not exposed).

Errors if the file cannot be opened or the format is unsupported. Note that pixel values are interpreted as sRGB by default; connect a linear-RGB conversion downstream if the file holds linear data like a normal or height map.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Path to an image file to load from disk.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Image decoded from the file on disk.

outputs.name: width
outputs.description: Width of the loaded image in pixels.

outputs.name: height
outputs.description: Height of the loaded image in pixels.

settings.name: from color
settings.description: Creates an image from a color.
settings.help:
Produces a 4-channel FloatImage where every pixel is the same color. Width and height are clamped to at least 1 and capped at 10000; the color is written directly as sRGB floats to avoid the precision loss of a u8 round-trip.

Handy for generating solid backgrounds, tinted fills for blend operations, or constant alpha masks. The color, width, and height are also passed through as separate outputs so downstream nodes can read the same values without re-wiring the original inputs.

inputs.name: color
inputs.description: Color used to fill every pixel of the output image.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description: Width of the generated image in pixels.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description: Height of the generated image in pixels.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Solid-color image filled with the chosen color.

outputs.name: color
outputs.description: Pass-through of the input color.

outputs.name: width
outputs.description: Final width of the generated image in pixels.

outputs.name: height
outputs.description: Final height of the generated image in pixels.

settings.name: line
settings.description: Generates a line shape as a grayscale SDF.
settings.help:
Draws an anti-aliased line segment from (start_x, start_y) to (end_x, end_y) with rounded caps, by evaluating the signed distance to the segment and smoothstepping the edge. Coordinates are 0-1 fractions of the canvas; thickness is in normalised units.

Output is a 1-channel FloatImage mask with 1.0 inside the stroke and 0.0 outside. When start and end coincide the node degrades gracefully into a filled disc centred on that point rather than producing an empty image.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description: Width of the generated image in pixels.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description: Height of the generated image in pixels.

inputs.name: start_x
inputs.description: Start-point X as a 0-1 fraction of image width.

inputs.name: start_y
inputs.description: Start-point Y as a 0-1 fraction of image height.

inputs.name: end_x
inputs.description: End-point X as a 0-1 fraction of image width.

inputs.name: end_y
inputs.description: End-point Y as a 0-1 fraction of image height.

inputs.name: thickness
inputs.description: Thickness of the line in normalised units.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Grayscale mask with the line drawn white on a black background.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: The transformed image, same size and channel count as the input.

settings.name: blend
settings.description: Blends an image onto another using a blend mode.
settings.help:
Walks every background pixel, samples the foreground at the same coordinate minus the position offset, and composites it using the selected BlendMode (Over, Lerp, Multiply, Overlay, SoftLight, and 12 more). Math is performed in the chosen ColorSpace; all 14 spaces (sRGB, Linear RGB, HSL, HSV, HWB, Lab, LCH, Oklab, Oklch, CMYK, XYZ, xyY, YCbCr, YUV) are supported and results are converted back to sRGBA for storage.

The amount input provides a global opacity; the alpha image (per-pixel, RGB channels averaged) multiplies that opacity so you can restrict the blend with a mask. Source alpha channels feed into the blend math. Foreground pixels outside the background's bounds leave the background untouched. Output size is taken from the background; positions may be negative to shift the foreground past the top-left edge.

inputs.name: background
inputs.description: Base image the foreground is composited onto; sets the output size.

inputs.name: foreground
inputs.description: Image composited on top of the background using the chosen blend mode.

inputs.name: amount
inputs.description: Global opacity applied to the blended foreground; 0 shows only the background.

inputs.name: alpha
inputs.description: Optional mask image; its per-pixel RGB average multiplies the blend amount.

inputs.name: blend mode
inputs.description: Compositing formula used to combine foreground and background.

inputs.name: color space
inputs.description: Colour space the blend math runs in (sRGB, Linear, HSL, Lab, etc.).

inputs.name: position x
inputs.description: Horizontal offset in pixels from the background's origin to place the foreground.

inputs.name: position y
inputs.description: Vertical offset in pixels from the background's origin to place the foreground.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Composited RGBA image sized to match the background.

Math is performed in the chosen ColorSpace; all 14 spaces (sRGB, Linear RGB, HSL, HSV, HWB, Lab, LCH, Oklab, Oklch, CMYK, XYZ, xyY, YCbCr, YUV) are supported and results are converted back to sRGBA for storage.

The amount input provides a global opacity; the alpha image (per-pixel, RGB channels averaged) multiplies that opacity so you can restrict the blend with a mask. Source alpha channels feed into the blend math. Foreground pixels outside the background's bounds leave the background untouched. Output size is taken from the background; positions may be negative to shift the foreground past the top-left edge.

inputs.name: background
inputs.description: Base image the foreground is composited onto; sets the output size.

inputs.name: foreground
inputs.description: Image composited on top of the background using the chosen blend mode.

inputs.name: amount
inputs.description: Global opacity applied to the blended foreground; 0 shows only the background.

inputs.name: alpha
inputs.description: Optional mask image; its per-pixel RGB average multiplies the blend amount.

inputs.name: blend mode
inputs.description: Compositing formula used to combine foreground and background.

inputs.name: color space
inputs.description: Colour space the blend math runs in (sRGB, Linear, HSL, Lab, etc.).

inputs.name: position x
inputs.description: Horizontal offset in pixels from the background's origin to place the foreground.

inputs.name: position y

settings.name: composite
settings.description: Pastes a foreground image onto a background at a given position.
settings.help:
Pastes the foreground onto the background using the standard Porter-Duff `over` operator: out_rgb = fg_rgb * fg_a + bg_rgb * (1 - fg_a), out_a = fg_a + bg_a * (1 - fg_a). The foreground's top-left lands at (position x, position y) relative to the background; pixels outside the background are skipped.

Works with any channel combination: missing alpha channels are treated as 1.0, grayscale inputs composite into the equivalent colour region. Will attempt to reuse the background's pixel buffer without cloning if it is the sole owner. Output matches the background's size and channel count exactly.

inputs.name: background
inputs.description: Base image the foreground is pasted onto; sets the output size.

inputs.name: foreground
inputs.description: Image alpha-composited on top of the background.

inputs.name: position x
inputs.description: Horizontal pixel offset of the foreground's top-left within the background.

inputs.name: position y
inputs.description: Vertical pixel offset of the foreground's top-left within the background.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Background with the foreground pasted in using standard over compositing.

settings.name: invert
settings.description: Inverts all color channels of an image.
settings.help:
Replaces each colour channel with 1 minus its value, producing a photographic negative. Alpha is skipped so transparency is preserved.

When the input image is the sole owner of its pixel data the node mutates in place instead of cloning, keeping it cheap in long chains. The operation assumes channels sit inside 0-1; values outside that interval are still inverted linearly but will remain outside 0-1, which downstream nodes may clip.

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Source image to produce a photographic negative of.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Image with each colour channel replaced by 1 minus its value; alpha preserved.
